papers.title_en: Convergence of Two-Timescale Markovian Stochastic Approximations with Applications in Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Vagul Mahadevan, Claire Chen, Shuze Daniel Liu, Shangtong Zhang
papers.evidence: 面向演员-评论家方法的两时间尺度收敛性分析
papers.tldr: 强化学习中的两时间尺度随机逼近算法（如TDC和actor-critic）在马尔可夫噪声下的收敛性长期未解。本文提出用慢尺度参数的运行最大值控制快尺度参数，替代传统方法，在不使用投影算子的条件下证明了稳定性和收敛性。作为应用，首次证明了带资格迹的TDC在离策略线性函数逼近下的几乎必然收敛。该工作为强化学习算法提供了更现实的收敛保证，拓展了理论分析框架。
papers.abstract_en: This work studies the convergence of two-timescale stochastic approximations (SA), a class of iterative algorithms that update two sets of parameters in fast and slow timescales respectively. Notable examples of two-timescale SA in reinforcement learning (RL) include temporal difference learning with gradient correction (TDC) and actor-critic methods. Previously, the stability (i.e., boundedness) and convergence of two-timescale SA were only established under i.i.d. noise. This work instead establishes the stability and convergence of two-timescale SA under Markovian noise, a setup that is more realistic in RL. Notably, we do not need to use any projection operator and the noise does not need to live in a compact space. Our key technical novelty is to control the fast timescale parameter with the running max of the slow timescale parameter, instead of with the current slow timescale parameter, as most prior works do. As a key application, we establish the first almost sure convergence of TDC with eligibility traces under off-policy learning with linear function approximation.
